rulings.Case Title: RAYNE VAN KING VS. STATE FARM LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defts To Appear At Depositions And Produce Documents;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, March 19, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF RAYNE KING's Motion To Compel Defendants To Appear At Depositions And Produce Documents Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA KEBEDE VS. FRANCIS H. CHUNG DDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton For Leave To Amend The Complaint To Sub As A Doe Deft
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 19, 2013, Line 9, PLAINTIFF SANDRA KEBEDE's motion For Leave To Amend The Complaint To Substitute As A Doe Defendant. The motion is granted under CCP 474. Plaintiff demonstrates that she was ignorant of Dr. Au's relationship to her injuries when she filed suit. CCP 474 is liberally construed and there is minimal prejudice to defendants because the trial is set for January 13, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS RUSH VS. WHITE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 19, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT TREADWELL & ROLLO, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrers to the causes of action are overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts that defendant actively concealed and/or conspired with the developer to actively conceal the soil tests. The Court must take those allegations as true on demurrer. rulings.Case Title: JON GRAY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 19, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT & SAN FRANCISCO POLICE COMMISSION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 30 days leave to amend. Mr. Gray has not pled sufficient facts to allege his causes of action for intentional tort, violation of City Charter Section 4.127, and SFPD's failure to comply with the application process. Mr. Gray uses the Judicial Council form complaint, but he has not included facts to support each cause of action to the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 19, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Judge Miller will disclose at the hearing that she was a partner at Morrison & Foerster from 1997 until February 2005 when she was appointed to the bench. Judge Miller occasionally worked on cases with Mr, Bennett. Judge Miller does not believe that this would affect her ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Continued to April 17, 2013 on the Court's own motion.
